decisions.title: Trial of Cannabidiol for Drug-Resistant Seizures in the Dravet Syndrome.
decisions.reasoning: The study is a Phase III double-blind placebo-controlled RCT that specifically enrolls patients with Dravet syndrome and evaluates the efficacy of cannabidiol, an antiseizure medication, against placebo. It reports primary randomized results on seizure frequency reduction and adverse events, satisfying all inclusion criteria.

decisions.title: Fenfluramine hydrochloride for the treatment of seizures in Dravet syndrome: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial.

It reports primary randomized results, including significant outcomes related to seizure frequency reduction, thus meeting all inclusion criteria.

decisions.title: Fenfluramine for Treatment-Resistant Seizures in Patients With Dravet Syndrome Receiving Stiripentol-Inclusive Regimens: A Randomized Clinical Trial.

decisions.title: Cannabis for paediatric epilepsy: challenges and conundrums.

decisions.title: Stiripentol: efficacy and tolerability in children with epilepsy.

decisions.title: Use of cannabidiol in the treatment of epilepsy.

decisions.title: Cannabidiol for drug-resistant seizures in the Dravet syndrome.

decisions.title: Fenfluramine treatment is associated with improvement in everyday executive function in preschool-aged children (<5 years) with Dravet syndrome: A critical period for early neurodevelopment.

decisions.title: Focal abnormalities detected by 18FDG PET in epileptic encephalopathies.

decisions.title: Randomized, dose-ranging safety trial of cannabidiol in Dravet syndrome.

decisions.title: Fenfluramine significantly reduces day-to-day seizure burden by increasing number of seizure-free days and time between seizures in patients with Dravet syndrome: A time-to-event analysis.

decisions.title: Time to onset of cannabidiol treatment effects in Dravet syndrome: Analysis from two randomized controlled trials.
